tweet: I hope everyone learns from today and from this entire year. We must stop the anger. We must condemn the violence. Stop the rhetoric.   May God help us and bless us in this endeavor.

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: But Congress doesn’t have the power, nor should it, to change election results — nor does an angry and violent mob.   I will vote today, tomorrow, whenever it happens, just as I planned to - for our Constitution and the preservation of our Republic.

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: This is not who we are. This is not how our American Democracy functions.   We are a nation of laws governed by the Constitution — not anarchy and insurrection.   My vote today was to be based on those principles. I don’t like the result of this election either.

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: This is un-American.   Mr. President, please do something.   Keep America safe. Enough.   Support our police.   This isn’t a protest. It’s anarchy.  I thought we were the party of law & order.

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: This. Is. Insane.

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: Just evacuated my office in Cannon due to a nearby threat. Now we’re seeing protesters assaulting Capitol Police.   This is wrong. This is not who we are. I’m heartbroken for our nation today.  https://t.co/jC9P0YfSLQ

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: The #Constitution isn’t worth the paper it’s written on if Members of Congress don’t respect it, and so much of what’s wrong in our country is because our leaders stopped respecting it long ago. I promised to change that, and that’s exactly what I’m going to do. (7/7)

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: If it could ignore the #ElectoralCollege, Joe Biden and Congressional Democrats could have claimed Russia rigged the 2016 election, without hard evidence, and use that as an excuse to put Hillary Clinton in the White House. (6/7)

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: But without action from the states, the #Constitution makes clear Congress MUST count the electors they’ve been sent. It cannot count electors that do not exist, and it can’t simply ignore the electors or their votes. (5/7)

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: State legislatures can audit their votes, investigate claims of criminality, and even appoint a whole new slate of electors to the #ElectoralCollege and ask Congress to count their votes rather than the votes of the original slate. (4/7)

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: When it comes to our elections, whether it be for Congress or President, the #Constitution is clear: The states rule. Outside of the powers given to Congress by the 15th, 19th, and 26th Amendments, the states are firmly in the driver’s seat. (3/7)

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: I understand the anger over the election results. I supported President Trump's re-election and believe he should've won. However, Congress has NO authority to overturn an election by rejecting states’ electors. Doing so violates our oath to the #Constitution. (2/7)

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: Over the course of the last several days, I’ve heard from countless people in the Lowcountry regarding the vote to certify the #ElectoralCollege. Like many of you, I’m gravely concerned about allegations of voter fraud in multiple states & believe this MUST be investigated. (1/7)

name: Rep. Nancy Mace
tweet: On Sunday I swore an oath to the Constitution. I promised to take that oath seriously. Period. I support the President, but the Constitution is clear: Congress may ONLY select new electors ONLY if states send new electors. More @postandcourier:  https://t.co/mIG89rq3fO
